name: Ham and Turkey Hash with Peppery Corn Muffins
recipeIngredient: 1/3 cup Cheddar Cheese, shredded, firmly packed
1 pound Sweet Potatoes, peeled and cut into 1-inch chunks
8 ounces Smoked Turkey Breasts, cut into 1-inch chunks
1 large Red Bell Pepper, cut into 1-inch chunks
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small saucepan over medium heat, melt the margarine; stir in flour; cook and stir for 1 minute. Add milk; bring to a boil, stirring frequently. Reduce heat to low; simmer about 2 minutes until thickened, stirring frequently. Remove from heat; stir in cheese and red pepper sauce until cheese is melted and mixture is smooth. Set sauce aside, covered.
2. In an ovenproof skillet over medium heat, heat oil. Add sweet potatoes; cook for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally until well browned and almost tender. Meanwhile, preheat the broiler.
3. To the skillet, add ham, turkey, and red bell pepper; cook for 6 to 8 minutes, stirring occasionally until meat is lightly browned. Gently stir in apples and scallions; cook for 2 to 3 minutes until apples are tender but still crisp.
4. Spoon the reserved cheese sauce over the hash in the skillet; broil about 4 inches from heat source for 3 to 4 minutes until sauce is lightly browned.
5. Serve accompanied by Peppery Corn Muffins.
Peppery Corn Muffins:
Preheat oven to 425°F. Spray a 10-inch muffin pan with no-stick cooking spray. Prepare the batter by spooning into prepared muffin pan cups, filling each cup about 3/4 full. Sprinkle batter in cups lightly with coarsely ground black pepper. Bake for 10 to 12 minutes until browned and a wooden pick inserted in the center of the muffin comes out clean.
